method.summary: Manual review of 55 high-ranking search results for the current test-post topic families. News-only pages and forums were avoided where a guide, blog, review, comparison, official source, or practical explainer was available.
method.rankingEvidence: Each page now stores serpEvidence with searchQuery, locale, device, observed position, capture timestamp, and reproducibility note. Rankings can vary, so this is evidence of the captured result set rather than a permanent ranking claim.
method.copyrightPolicy: Stored only structural observations, not article bodies.

repeatedWinningBlocks: Answer or verdict appears before deep explanation
Updated date, author/reviewer, and trust/method note appear near the top
Jump links or sticky contents reduce scanning effort
Comparison tables or specs tables appear before or beside long prose
Images are real product/context images with captions
Sources and methodology are separated from public competitor-analysis notes
Policy/tax/admission topics lead with official sources
Mobile pages keep the answer and checklist before secondary navigation
avoid: Public SERP-analysis language such as top-page format notes
Too many internal research links in public article pages
Unsupported superlatives, copied competitor wording, or hidden source context
Empty card grids, decorative complexity, and large blocks before the answer
